column2: The first phase of the data lifecycle; involves generation or acquisition of new data.
column3: Data Lifecycle, Create Phase, Data Generation

column2: Lifecycle phase for data at rest; involves encryption and physical protection.
column3: Data Lifecycle, Store Phase, Data at Rest

column2: Lifecycle phase where data is actively processed in memory or on screen.
column3: Data Lifecycle, Use Phase, Data in Use

column2: Lifecycle phase where data is transmitted (data in transit); protected by TLS or DLP.
column3: Data Lifecycle, Share Phase, Data in Transit

column2: Long-term storage of data no longer in active use; addresses media obsolescence.
column3: Data Lifecycle, Archive Phase, Media Obsolescence

column2: The final phase involving sanitization to prevent data remanence.
column3: Data Lifecycle, Destroy Phase, Media Sanitization

column2: Sanitization level using software to overwrite data; protects against simple recovery.
Advanced sanitization (e.g., Secure Erase) protecting against laboratory recovery.
Physical destruction (shredding, incineration) of media; the most secure method.
Using strong magnets to destroy data on magnetic media (ineffective for SSDs/CDs).
De-identifying data by destroying its encryption keys; essential for cloud data.
The residual representation of data remaining on media after sanitization attempts.
column3: Data Lifecycle, Data Remanence, Media Sanitization

column2: Requirement to suspend data destruction when litigation is anticipated.
System that monitors and blocks unauthorized transfers of sensitive data.
column3: Data Security, DLP, Data in Transit

column2: Cloud Access Security Broker; enforces security policies for cloud-based data.
Persistent protection that controls file usage (copy/paste, print) regardless of location.
Hardware Security Module; a physical device that manages and stores encryption keys.
Data stored on persistent media (hard drives, tapes).
column3: Data States, Data at Rest, Encryption

column2: Data moving across a network (email, web traffic, SFTP).
column3: Data States, Data in Transit, TLS

column2: Data being actively processed in RAM or CPU caches.
column3: Data States, Data in Use, Memory Protection

column2: SSD feature that can hide data from software-based overwriting tools.
The primary US government guideline for media sanitization.
The process of identifying, collecting, and producing electronic data for legal cases.
Electronic Discovery Reference Model; the standard framework for e-discovery.
Capability to remotely sanitize a lost or stolen mobile device.
column3: Mobile Security, Remote Wipe, Media Sanitization
